note: The about:gjoa settings registry. gjoa's single settings home. Each setting is a live pref (present + reversible). Privacy profiles are CURATED presets — pick one and it flips a curated subset of the granular toggles; hand-editing any toggle switches you to Custom. Costs are MEASURED, characterized as real EGRESS endpoints, or honestly 'unmeasured' — never invented. Profile `sets` are kept in sync with GjoaLoader's PRIVACY-PRESETS by preflight Gate N. Firefox's own settings stay in Firefox Settings; this is gjoa's surface.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.intro: Pick a profile, or tune the individual toggles below (which switches you to Custom). Profiles escalate — each is a superset of the one before it.

profiles.title: Minimal
profiles.summary: gjoa's privacy defaults — telemetry and phone-home off, Do-Not-Track on. Zero site breakage.

profiles.title: Balanced
profiles.summary: Minimal + trims cross-site referers and turns off search / URL-bar suggestions (no keystroke leak to your search engine). Negligible breakage.

profiles.title: LibreWolf-Inspired
profiles.asterisk: A curated subset of LibreWolf's privacy DEFAULTS — not LibreWolf itself: its compile-time disables and ~20 C++ patches aren't here, and this changes none of gjoa's own added surface (the adblock classifier, content actors, about: pages).
profiles.summary: Balanced + resistFingerprinting (canvas / timezone / UA spoofing + window letterboxing), strict referer policy, and extension auto-update off. Real UX cost: RFP breaks some sites.

granular.title: Resist fingerprinting (RFP)
granular.help: Spoofs canvas, timezone (UTC), and a generic UA to defeat fingerprinting. High UX cost — breaks canvas-reliant sites.

granular.title: Letterboxing
granular.help: Pads the viewport to a generic size so window dimensions can't fingerprint you. Visible grey margins.

granular.title: Cross-origin referer policy
granular.help: 0 = send full referer; 2 = send only on same-origin requests (strictest).

granular.title: Cross-origin referer trimming
granular.help: 0 = full URL; 2 = trim cross-origin referer to scheme+host+port (no path/query leak).

granular.title: Search suggestions
granular.help: Off stops sending what you type to the search engine for suggestions.

granular.title: URL-bar search suggestions
granular.help: Off stops URL-bar keystrokes reaching the search engine.

granular.title: Extension auto-update
granular.help: Off stops automatic add-on update checks (an egress + supply-chain consideration). You update manually.

granular.title: Do-Not-Track header
granular.help: Sends DNT:1. Advisory only — most sites ignore it; gjoa's native blocking is what actually stops trackers.

sections.title: Dark mode & color
sections.intro: Everything about how gjoa colors the web and dresses its own chrome — the dark-mode engine plus the gjoa theme — in one place. gjoa is dark-first: by default a bright white site (think YouTube) is darkened for you. Two groups below: Dark mode (how web pages are darkened) and Theme (gjoa's own window colors).

subsections.title: Dark mode
subsections.intro: Engine-level dark mode. Sites that ship their own dark theme can render it; the rest are inverted at style-resolution time (cached in the engine, no per-frame compositor filter). The layers stack, broadest to narrowest: the master switch → the Appearance (System / Dark / Light) → the engine appearance knobs (Darkness, Text brightness, Scrim) → the three Per-site lists → gjoa's built-in curated per-site fixes. Each narrower layer overrides the one before it. The three Per-site lists apply only in the 'Dark' appearance (System and Light add no inversion). Everything is right here — nothing lives in about:config.

settings.title: Dark mode (master switch)
settings.help: The on/off for gjoa's whole dark-mode engine. When ON, the Mode below decides how each page is darkened. When OFF, gjoa does nothing to page colors — every site renders in its own authored colors (usually light), exactly like vanilla Firefox.

examples.label: ON
examples.detail: Reddit, YouTube, and a plain white blog are all darkened according to the Mode you pick below.

examples.label: OFF
examples.detail: YouTube is bright white, Reddit is its normal light theme — gjoa stays out of the way.

settings.title: Appearance
settings.help: How web pages look — the universal three-way control (like Chrome/Firefox/Safari, but dark-first). System follows your OS theme and lets each site decide. Dark makes every site dark — native-dark sites keep their own theme, the rest are engine-inverted into your Darkness band with bright hero banners/photos toned down. Light keeps every site in its light theme. Only matters while the master switch is ON; the Per-site lists below apply in Dark.

examples.label: System — follow my OS
examples.detail: Conventional browser behavior — exactly what Chrome, Firefox, and Safari mean. Content follows your real OS theme both ways and each site decides via prefers-color-scheme; gjoa adds ZERO inversion. On a light OS a themeless site stays bright, by design. Pick this to make gjoa behave like a stock browser.

examples.label: Dark (default)
examples.detail: gjoa's signature. Every site reads dark on any OS, no white flash. A site with its OWN good dark theme (GitHub, Reddit, signed-in YouTube) keeps that theme; a light or themeless site is engine-inverted into your Darkness band, and bright hero banners/photos are toned down so nothing stays glaringly light. The ONLY appearance where the three Per-site lists below apply.

examples.label: Light
examples.detail: Force every site to its light theme on any OS — gjoa adds no darkening. A quick way to step out of dark for a site or a session without turning the whole engine off.

settings.title: Darkness
settings.help: How dark inverted (engine-darkened) pages get — the page-background lightness floor every darkened site lands on. 0–100; LOWER = darker. The default 16 is a deep low-grey (about #0d0d0d), deliberately not pure black. Raise it if dark feels too stark; lower it toward 8–10 for near-black.

examples.label: 16 (default)
examples.detail: Page backgrounds settle on a deep #0d0d0d — dark and uniform without crushing to black.
Pushes backgrounds toward near-black (#020202) — maximum contrast, can feel harsh.
A lighter, dim-grey page (~#2a2a2a) — gentler if pure-dark feels too stark.

settings.note: Only affects pages the engine actually inverts. A site keeping its OWN dark theme (Reddit, or any native-dark site under system/hybrid) ignores this slider — its darkness comes from the site, not gjoa.

settings.title: Text brightness ceiling
settings.help: The companion to Darkness: the brightest result the inverter will produce, i.e. how light the text and light surfaces get on a darkened page. 0–100; HIGHER = brighter text. The default ~92 is a soft off-white (about #e4e4e4), deliberately not pure #fff (full white glares on a dark backdrop).

examples.label: 92 (default)
examples.detail: Body text renders as a comfortable off-white — high legibility without glare.
Pure-white text — maximum contrast, sharper but harsher on a dark page.
Dimmer, grey-ish text — softer, lower-contrast; good in a very dark room.

settings.note: Most people only ever touch Darkness; this is the advanced ceiling for the same OKLCH lightness band. Only applies to engine-inverted pages.

settings.title: Photo scrim strength
settings.help: On an inverted page, full-bleed background photos (a hero banner) can't be inverted without looking wrong, so gjoa lays a translucent black scrim over them — this keeps any preserved (un-inverted) text readable against the still-bright photo. 0–255; HIGHER = darker scrim. 0 turns the scrim off entirely.

examples.label: 140 (default)
examples.detail: A site with a bright hero image gets a moderate dark wash so white caption text over it stays readable.
No scrim — hero photos render at full brightness; text over them may wash out.
Heavy near-opaque wash — photos are strongly dimmed, text maximally legible.

settings.title: Per-image refinement (experimental)
settings.help: An optional second pass that rasterizes each visible background image, classifies its brightness, and refines it individually (hides large light backdrops, inverts small dark icons, etc.). OFF by default — it is unverified polish layered on top of the scrim, which already handles the common case.

examples.label: OFF (default)
examples.detail: The engine scrim handles background images — correct for nearly every page, zero extra work.

examples.label: ON
examples.detail: gjoa additionally inspects each image; may improve odd icon/logo edge cases, but can occasionally misjudge an image. Turn on only if a specific site's images look wrong.

settings.title: Per-site — keep the site's own theme
settings.help: Comma-separated domains gjoa should NEVER invert — it leaves them in their own authored colors (their native dark theme if they have one, otherwise light). Use this for a site whose own dark theme you prefer over gjoa's inversion.

examples.label: github.com, news.ycombinator.com
examples.detail: GitHub keeps its own dark theme; HN renders as-authored — gjoa won't touch either, while every other site still follows your Mode.

examples.label: (empty — default)
examples.detail: No exceptions; every site follows the Mode above.

settings.note: Overrides gjoa's automatic per-site decision. Lowest of the three per-site rules: 'disable' wins, then 'force dark', then this.

settings.title: Per-site — always force gjoa dark
settings.help: Comma-separated domains gjoa should ALWAYS engine-invert into your Darkness band — even if the site ships its own dark theme. Use this when a site's native dark theme is ugly or inconsistent and you'd rather have gjoa's uniform dark.

examples.label: reddit.com
examples.detail: Instead of Reddit's near-black native theme, gjoa inverts it to your Darkness setting (e.g. #0d0d0d), consistent with every other inverted site.

examples.label: (empty — default)
examples.detail: No forced sites; gjoa decides per the Mode above.

settings.note: Beats 'keep the site's own theme', but is itself overridden by 'disable' below.

settings.title: Per-site — disable dark mode entirely
settings.help: Comma-separated domains where gjoa does NOTHING — the page renders exactly as authored (usually light), as if dark mode were off, but only for these sites. Use this for a site where any darkening looks broken (design tools, maps, color-critical work).

examples.label: figma.com, maps.google.com
examples.detail: Design / map tools render in true colors, untouched, while every other site stays dark.

examples.label: (empty — default)
examples.detail: No sites excluded.

settings.note: Highest-precedence per-site rule — beats both lists above.

settings.title: Contrast guard (experimental)
settings.help: A final readability pass: after inversion settles, gjoa measures real text against its real background and re-tones any text that would fall below a legibility floor — the 'never unreadable, no black-on-black' backstop. OFF by default pending full build verification; the engine inversion already yields readable contrast in the common case.

examples.label: OFF (default)
examples.detail: Engine inversion handles contrast — correct for nearly every page, zero extra work.

examples.label: ON
examples.detail: gjoa additionally re-tones any low-contrast text it detects. Turn on if a darkened site has hard-to-read text.

settings.title: Contrast guard strictness
settings.help: How strict the Contrast guard is, in APCA Lc (perceptual contrast). HIGHER = stricter (more text gets re-toned). ~45 ≈ large / secondary text; raise toward 60 for stricter body-text contrast. Only matters when the Contrast guard above is ON.

examples.label: 45 (default)
examples.detail: Catches genuinely illegible text without over-correcting.
Stricter — enforces stronger body-text contrast, re-tones more aggressively.

subsections.title: Theme
subsections.intro: gjoa's OWN window chrome — the toolbar, tabs, and menus — not web-page colors. One OKLCH token system, fed by a base16 palette. Pick gjoa's canonical look, a curated variant, or dissolve into your desktop. No restart needed.

settings.title: gjoa theme
settings.help: Which palette paints gjoa's chrome. Changes apply live.

examples.label: Polar Night
examples.detail: gjoa's signature: the deep polar-night sea, arctic-ice surfaces, aurora-cyan accent. The default identity.

examples.label: Nord
examples.detail: The well-known Nord palette, normalized through gjoa's same token system — a cooler, softer cousin.

examples.label: System
examples.detail: Routes gjoa's chrome colors through your desktop / Firefox theme (stylix, an installed theme, or OS colors) so the browser dissolves into your setup. This also flips the gjoa.theme.useSystem switch below.

settings.title: Use system / desktop colors
settings.help: Routes the gjoa palette through Firefox's theme variables (stylix, an installed theme, OS colors) instead of gjoa's own. The 'System' theme above turns this ON; any other variant turns it OFF — so normally you just pick the theme above and leave this alone. Exposed here for transparency and manual control.

examples.label: OFF (default)
examples.detail: gjoa paints its own canonical chrome colors (Polar Night or Nord).

examples.label: ON
examples.detail: gjoa borrows your system/Firefox theme colors — equivalent to choosing the 'System' theme above.

settings.title: Allow-list (hosts)
settings.help: Comma-separated hosts to exempt from blocking (your own allow-list). Empty = block everywhere.

settings.title: List-driven scriptlets
settings.help: SECURITY: off = only curated baseline scriptlets run; on allows arbitrary +js() injection from your filter lists. Leave off unless you trust your lists.

measurement.basis: A security gate (finding F2), not a perf knob.

sections.title: Reversible features (debloat)
sections.intro: Firefox subsystems gjoa disables by default - present and reversible. gjoa never deletes a capability; it parks it behind a knob. These are EGRESS/feature toggles, not memory knobs - so the honest cost is the network endpoint each would re-contact if you toggle it on (documented Firefox behaviour), not an invented perf number. Idle-memory deltas remain unmeasured; they are not the relevant axis here.

measurement.basis: Firefox submits telemetry pings to incoming.telemetry.mozilla.org. Off in gjoa, so it never fires; toggling on re-enables that egress. Idle CPU/memory of the telemetry subsystem is unmeasured (not the relevant cost).
Firefox Health Report uploads to incoming.telemetry.mozilla.org. The cost is the data sent, not memory.

Pocket fetches recommendations/sponsored content from getpocket endpoints (surfaced on the stock new-tab, which gjoa replaces anyway). Feature toggle; no saving exists for a feature that was never running.
Disables Sync (accounts.firefox.com). Feature loss, not a measured perf win - egress only occurs if you actively sign in.
The add-ons recommendation service (TAAR) sends usage signals to services.addons.mozilla.org. Egress vector; memory cost unmeasured.
Normandy pulls remote experiment/recipe definitions from normandy.cdn.mozilla.net and can flip prefs remotely. Disabling it removes both that egress and remote mutation of your build.
Sponsored-tile placement is fetched from contile.services.mozilla.com. No functional cost (custom new-tab); the cost is the egress.

sections.title: Layout
sections.intro: How gjoa arranges tabs and chrome. Nothing here lives in about:config — every layout knob is below.

settings.title: Dynamic orientation (width-responsive tabs)
settings.help: When ON, gjoa auto-switches between vertical tabs (sidebar) on a wide window and horizontal tabs on a narrow / half-width one, live as you resize. A manual mode switch is honoured until the width next crosses the threshold below.

examples.label: ON (default)
examples.detail: Tile gjoa to half your screen and tabs flow horizontal; widen it and they return to the sidebar — automatically.

examples.label: OFF
examples.detail: gjoa stays in whatever tab orientation you last chose, regardless of window width.

measurement.basis: Auto-switches to horizontal tabs on a narrow / half-width window and vertical (sidebar) tabs on a wide one, live as the window resizes. No egress; pure layout.

settings.title: Narrow-width threshold (px)
settings.help: The window inner-width, in pixels, at or below which Dynamic orientation flips to horizontal tabs. ~half of a common 1920px display. Only has an effect while Dynamic orientation (above) is ON; tune it for your display or tiling setup.

examples.label: 1000 (default)
examples.detail: Windows narrower than ~1000px get horizontal tabs; wider stays vertical — a good half-of-1920 split.
Switches to horizontal sooner (at a wider window) — good if you want the sidebar only on large windows.
Keeps the vertical sidebar down to quite narrow windows before flipping horizontal.

settings.note: Ignored when Dynamic orientation is OFF.

settings.title: Horizontal tabs — single-row style
settings.help: Chooses the STYLE of horizontal tabs. OFF = the classic layout (toolbar on top, tabs in their own strip below it). ON = a single combined row where the toolbar icons collapse into the omni-icon menu, the URL bar grows, and tabs flow on the same row: [menu][url][tab1 … tabN]. Only affects horizontal mode — vertical (sidebar) tabs are unchanged.

examples.label: OFF — classic (default)
examples.detail: Two rows in horizontal mode: the toolbar, then a dedicated tab strip beneath it.

examples.label: ON — single row
examples.detail: Everything on one line to save vertical space: omni-menu, a wide URL bar, then the tabs — compact, good on short windows.

settings.note: No effect in vertical / sidebar tab mode.

settings.title: Horizontal tabs — sidebar launcher strip
settings.help: Controls Firefox's vertical sidebar launcher strip (the narrow left column of icons: AI/sparkle, sidebar, history, bookmarks, settings) WHILE in horizontal tab mode. OFF (default) hides it — the tabs already live in the toolbar row, so the strip is redundant chrome, and the content reclaims the column. ON brings it back. Toggle live with leader-b or the `:sidebar-strip` command. Only affects horizontal mode — in vertical / sidebar tabs the strip always shows.

examples.label: OFF — hidden (default)
examples.detail: In horizontal mode the left icon strip is gone and web content fills the freed width. Toggle on the fly with leader-b.

examples.label: ON — shown
examples.detail: Keeps the vertical launcher strip (AI, sidebar, history, bookmarks, settings icons) visible even in horizontal mode, as stock Firefox does.

settings.note: No effect in vertical / sidebar tab mode — that mode always shows the strip.

links.title: Sovereignty - egress proof
links.desc: The source-derived list of every point this build reaches the network, checked against the build you're running. A proof, not a setting.

links.title: Raw preferences
links.desc: Every gjoa.* and Firefox pref, raw. The expert escape hatch - each setting above shows its pref name to search here.
